trialDescriptions.title: Gewichtsmanagement mit einer digitalen Lebensstilintervention bei Personen mit Adipositas
trialDescriptions.summary: Übergewicht und Adipositas sind eine große gesundheitliche Herausforderung. Immer mehr digitale Programme zur Gewichtsreduktion werden auf dem Markt angeboten. Die Effektivität solcher Programme ist ziemlich unklar. Deshalb soll die Wirkung eines App-basierten Lebensstilinterventionsprogramm untersucht werden.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary: Wissenschaftliche Daten zur Effektivität app-basierter Interventionen zur Gewichtsreduktion sind begrenzt, vor allem für Deutschland. Studien konnten die Effektivität von Mobiltelefon- und app-basierten Interventionen bzgl. gewichtsbezogenen Endpunkten zeigen. In dieser Studie soll die Effektivität einer App zur Gewichtsreduktion nach 12 Wochen Intervention untersucht werden. Zusätzlich sollen die Effektivität nach 24 Wochen, die Lebensqualität sowie die Benutzerfreundlichkeit und -akzeptanz der App untersucht werden.

trialDescriptions.title: Weight management with a digital lifestyle intervention in persons with obesity
trialDescriptions.summary: Overweight and obesity are a major health threat. More and more digital weight loss programs are offered on the market. However, the effectiveness of such programs is unclear. Therefore, the effect of an app-based lifestyle intervention program on weight loss will be investigated.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary: Scientific data on the effectiveness of app-based weight loss interventions are scarce, especially for Germany. Studies have shown the effectiveness of cell phone and app-based interventions regarding weight-related outcomes. In this study, the effectiveness of an app for weight reduction will be investigated after 12 weeks of use. In addition, the effectiveness after 24 weeks, quality of life and usability and user acceptance of the app will be investigated.

observationalGroups.comment: ADHOC-Gruppe: App-basierte Lebensstilintervention für 12 Wochen, im Anschluss 12 Wochen Nachbeobachtungszeit
ADHOC group: app-based lifestyle intervention for 12 weeks, followed by 12 weeks of follow-up
EXPECT-Gruppe: Bisheriger Lebensstil für 12 Wochen, im Anschluss App-basierte Lebensstilintervention für 12 Wochen
EXPECT group: current lifestyle for 12 weeks, followed by 12 weeks of app-based lifestyle intervention

characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: Änderung des Körpergewichts nach 24 Wochen; Änderung der Lebensqualität (EQ-5D-5L) nach 12 und 24 Wochen; Benutzerfreundlichkeit und -akzeptanz (standardisierte Fragen) der App

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: Change of body weight after 12 weeks
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: Change of body weight after 24 weeks; change of quality of life (EQ-5D-5L) after 12 and 24 weeks; usability and user acceptance (standardized questions) of the app

trialResultsDescriptions.ipdSharingPlanDescription: Die Daten werden ausschließlich zur Untersuchung der Effektivität der eingesetzten App erhoben und ausgewertet.
trialResultsDescriptions.briefSummaryOfResultsDescription:
Background: Despite an increasing number of smartphone applications (apps) addressing weight management, data on the effect of app-based multimodal obesity treatment approaches on weight loss is limited. This study aimed to examine the effect of a digital multimodal weight loss intervention program delivered by an app on body weight in persons with obesity.

Methods: For this single-centre randomized controlled study, 168 adults with a body mass index (BMI) between 30.0 and 40.0 kg/m2 without severe comorbidities were recruited in the region of Munich and randomized into two intervention groups. The ADHOC group received an app-based multimodal weight loss program from baseline on for 12 weeks plus 12 weeks of follow-up. The EXPECT group received the app-based intervention for 12 weeks after 12 weeks of "waiting" (no intervention). Anthropometric data, data on quality of life (EuroQol, EQ-5D-5L), and app usage data were collected.

Results: 64.3% of study participants were women, mean age was 46.8 ± 11.0 years, and mean BMI was 34.2 ± 2.8 kg/m2. The completers analysis resulted in a weight loss of 3.2 ± 3.2 kg (3.2 ± 3.0%) in the ADHOC group and 0.4 ± 2.6 kg (0.3 ± 2.6%) in the EXPECT group after 12 weeks, with a significant difference between the groups (β [95% CI] = -2.9 [-3.8; -1.9], p < 0.001). Completers in the ADHOC group showed weight maintenance after 24 weeks. The time spent on the app was associated with weight reduction (β [95% CI] = -0.10 [-0.18; -0.01], p = 0.03).

Conclusions: Application of a multimodal app-based weight loss program results in moderate weight loss in persons with obesity.

trialResultsDescriptions.ipdSharingPlanDescription: The data is collected and analyzed exclusively for the purpose of investigating the effectiveness of the app used.
trialResultsDescriptions.briefSummaryOfResultsDescription:

publications.description: Gemesi K, Winkler S, Schmidt-Tesch S, Schederecker F, Hauner H, Holzapfel C. Efficacy of an app-based multimodal lifestyle intervention on body weight in persons with obesity: results from a randomized controlled trial. Int J Obes (Lond). 2024 Jan;48(1):118-126. doi: 10.1038/s41366-023-01415-0. Epub 2023 Nov 28. PMID: 38017117; PMCID: PMC10746538.

institutes.name: Technische Universität München, Institut für Ernährungsmedizin

recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria: Body Mass Index (BMI) 30 bis 40 kg/m²; Besitz eines Smartphones

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria: Bariatric surgery, pregnancy, lactation, severe diseases
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria: Body Mass Index (BMI) between 30 and 40 kg/m², ownership of a smartphone

contact.affiliation: Ethikkommission der Fakultät für Medizin der Technischen Universität München
